It will be interesting to see whether this movie puts as much emphasis on mircales as the preview does. We've moved to the point in our culture that we no longer regard miracles as a meaningful contribution to the conversation about God. We used to hear atheists folding their arms and trying to "explain" them in other ways, as though what mattered was the question of whether or not they occurred. But this was the wrong question. The question is whether a person performing miracles is necessarily good. Why would God want to teach us by illustrating that the laws of nature can be suspended? Why would the "Good News" — which that we can all be forgiven for our sins — be accompanied by a multiplication of fishes and loaves, changing water into wine or wine into blood, walking on water, or a reversal of disease or death? If I were there, I'd surely be impressed, but if I were already convinced by the philosophy, why would I need a magic trick? 

Is it so that I know the philosophy comes from God, from someone who created nature and can therefore defy it? That's an interesting idea. But if the goal was to persuade me that God is behind the philosophy, why wouldn't God speak to us more directly? He seems to want to have persuaded a few people -- coincidentally, by the way, they were all illiterate, if this did occur, because no one wrote down their experiences, even though writing and reading was very popular at that time. It's curious that he he didn't want writers to see the miracles. And it's curious that he didn't speak to the apostles and the writers of the Gospel *together*, so that they'd be accountable to the message, based on others having heard it at the same time. But ok, never mind, God works in mysterious ways.

But then there's a second problem. If he's being mysterious, just showing a few illiterate people and making sure there's lots of disagreement about what was seeing - and then kind of shutting down the conversation by speaking to us again - don't we have to consider that these miracles could have been corrupt? Why would I think a person was better than me, merely because they were more powerful?
